Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix EPERM crash on reboot: add entitlements + fix rate-limit validation

- Remove custom keyGenerator in tictalk.ts that triggered express-rate-limit

- Add Medusa.entitlements with file access, network, and JIT permissions
  so macOS doesn't block the ad-hoc signed app after reboot
- Update build-app.sh to embed entitlements during code signing

commit.message:
fix: use privileged:true on dd-agent instead of cgroupns_mode

cgroupns_mode is rejected by older Docker Compose versions (v2.7.0).
privileged:true is universally supported and also solves the cgroup v2
"failed to register process metrics" error that prevents the OTLP
ingest pipeline from binding port 4317.

docs(template): add Action Item Ledger to handoff template (#90)

* docs(template): add Action Item Ledger to session handoff template

Session audit found 57% action item drop rate across 13 global sessions.
Root cause: handoff template had no carry-forward mechanism — items
silently disappeared between sessions.

Adds Action Item Ledger section requiring explicit status (done/carried/
deferred/dropped/reassigned) for every prior item. Implementation notes
cover ledger lifecycle pruning, global session scanning across project
handoffs, and archived project handling.

Closes the primary gap in session continuity tracking.

Session 16: Reference data (IRENA/CRREM/Grid EF) + Violation Tracker ingesters & API

A13 — IRENA LCOE + CRREM Pathways + Grid Emission Factors:
- Combined reference data ingester for three benchmark datasets
- IRENA: 8 renewable technologies x 9 years LCOE (USD/MWh) with learning curves
- CRREM v2.0: 7 property types x 9 countries x 2 scenarios (1.5C/2C) x 31 years
  energy intensity pathways (kWh/m2) with stranding risk classification
- Grid EFs: 20 countries x 6 years (kgCO2/kWh) from Ember/IEA data
- 4,098 total reference data records loaded

A10 — Violation Tracker scraper:
- Corporate penalty/violation ingester from Good Jobs First Violation Tracker
- 18 curated sample violations across Oil & Gas, Mining, Financial Services,
  Technology, Chemicals, Commodities, Food & Beverage sectors
- Covers EPA, SEC, DOJ, OSHA, CFPB, FCA, FTC and international agencies
- Penalties range from $60K (OSHA) to $20.8B (Deepwater Horizon)

Migration 031: 4 new tables
- dh_irena_lcoe (technology, LCOE, capacity factor, installed cost)
- dh_crrem_pathways (property type, scenario, energy/carbon intensity)
- dh_grid_emission_factors (country-year grid EF with generation mix)
- dh_violation_tracker (company, type, agency, penalty, severity, status)

Serve layer: 17 new API endpoints
- /api/v1/reference-data: IRENA LCOE search/technologies/trend, CRREM
  search/property-types/pathway, Grid EF search/countries/{iso3}, stats
- /api/v1/violations: search, companies, types, agencies, sectors,
  company/{name}, stats

Smoke tests: 38/38 passing across 8 test groups
Total ingesters: 12 registered

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(molt-stdlib): replace globals() with sys.modules dict in 22 stdlib files

In compiled Molt binaries, globals() returns a detached empty dict because
the compiled binary doesn't push frame entries during stdlib module init.
This breaks dynamic attribute injection, module dict reads, and namespace
cleanup across many stdlib modules.

Fix: use sys.modules[__name__].__dict__ with globals() fallback for all
non-intrinsic globals() patterns — .get() reads, .update()/.[]= writes,
.pop() cleanup, and for-in iteration.

Files fixed: enum, selectors, ctypes/_endian, ctypes/wintypes, sys, signal,
subprocess, os, _thread, asyncio (2), concurrent/futures, importlib (5),
runpy, tkinter/constants, test, test/support.

Also adds src/molt/stdlib/** to WASM CI trigger paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hold-short placement closer to runway, RWY edges follow centerline

- Hold-short distance now uses halfWidth + 75ft buffer from runway edge
  instead of FAA AC 150/5300-13B Table 3-2 (125-300ft from centerline).
  Prevents hold-short nodes from landing near taxiway junctions.

- ConnectOnRunwayNodes now walks on-runway node chains to find the node
  closest to the runway centerline, instead of using the HS node's
  immediate neighbor. Fixes diagonal RWY edges (e.g. P/28L at OAK where
  node 371 was used instead of centerline node 370).

- Add ground view debug overlay (Ctrl+D): shows node IDs, types, and
  edge names with endpoint IDs for all nodes and edges.

commit.message:
fix: auto-open guide via useEffect instead of useState initial value

useState only uses its initial value on first mount. Since SetupGuide
lives in the root layout (already mounted), the ?guide=open param
was ignored. Now useEffect reacts to the param change and expands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: prune devDeps before electron-builder to fix OOM during module scan

electron-builder was scanning the full node_modules (including all
devDependencies) and hitting the 7GB runner memory limit. Build first,
prune devDeps, then package so the scan is dramatically smaller.

commit.message:
Reject plans without checkbox-format tasks

Interactive planning can produce prose documents (architecture summaries,
bullet points) instead of the required `- [ ]` checkbox format. Ralph
silently accepted these as valid 0-task plans, then immediately marked
status as "completed" since there were no remaining tasks.

- Validate task count after both interactive and autonomous planning
- Delete invalid plan files and exit with actionable error message
- Guard execution loop: 0 total tasks on first iteration is an error

Add smart cost lookup with fallback matching in estimate engine

errors when template material names don't exactly match cost database entries.

commit.message:
Fix Railway crash: create database directory before sqlite3.connect

Railway sets DATABASE_PATH=/data/dispatch.db but the /data directory
does not exist until the volume is mounted. Added os.makedirs() call
in both app.py and import_csv.py to create the parent directory before
any database connection attempt. Also simplified the default fallback
to a relative path for cleaner local development.

Fixes: sqlite3.OperationalError: unable to open database file

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix email notifications: await instead of fire-and-forget

Vercel serverless functions terminate immediately after the response
is returned, so the fire-and-forget async IIFE never executed. Now
the email send is awaited before returning the 201 response, wrapped
in try/catch so email failures never block message delivery.

Phase 10.5 Step 10: update full test suite for RBAC

- apps/core/tests.py (new): RBAC unit tests
  * has_role() returns True/False for assigned/unassigned roles
  * has_permission() reflects union of all role permissions
  * Multiple roles give union of permissions
  * Role convenience properties (is_supplier_admin, is_payroll_reviewer)
  * create_saas_admin command creates user with saas_admin role + is_staff
  * create_saas_admin command is idempotent for existing usernames

  Interactive management command to create SaaS Admin accounts
- apps/events/tests.py: new Ok to Pay and Payroll Reviewer tests
  * OkToPayTransitionTest: Complete → Ok to Pay (permitted/blocked)
  * Revert Ok to Pay → Complete (permitted/blocked)
  * Payroll Reviewer can mark Ok to Pay (coverage area required)
  * PayrollReviewerVisibilityTest: sees events in coverage area
    all statuses including Draft, Ok to Pay; blocked outside coverage

188 tests, all passing.

commit.message:
Add full ticket system: replies, close, edit, numbering

Models:
- DirectorMessage: add status, reply, replied_at, reply_read, updated_at fields
- ticket_number property (#0001), is_open, has_reply

Views:
- ticket_list: list tickets (creator sees all, director sees own)
- ticket_detail: view ticket + reply form for creator
- send_message: create new ticket, redirect to ticket_detail
- edit_ticket: director edits message (open + no reply only)
- reply_ticket: creator writes reply
- close_ticket: creator closes ticket (director can only view)

Templates:
- ticket_list.html: table with badge for unread/replied/status
- ticket_detail.html: full conversation view with actions
- send_message.html: reused for create + edit modes

Sidebar: show unread reply badge for directors

commit.message:
fix: use raw https.get for header fallback to bypass TLS cert mismatch

When scanning from the production server, the IP-based header fallback
was failing because fetch() validates TLS certs and the cert doesn't
match the raw IP address. Switch to Node.js https.get with
rejectUnauthorized:false for the fallback request. This correctly
detects nginx-added headers like X-Content-Type-Options when the
primary fetch bypasses the reverse proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SSL certificate verification for ONTAP connections

Issue: Node.js fetch (undici) doesn't support the agent option for SSL
Solution: Set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 when ONTAP_INSECURE=true

Changes:
- Remove https.Agent (not compatible with built-in fetch)
- Set process.env.NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED when ONTAP_INSECURE is true
- Simplify fetch options (no agent parameter needed)
- Add warning message when TLS verification is disabled

This allows the server to connect to ONTAP clusters with self-signed
certificates when ONTAP_INSECURE=true is set in the environment file.

commit.message:
fix: patch dual-DB implementation for production use

Four bugs fixed in db-dual-init.js:

1. Statement.run() now calls saveDbFile() after every write so
   in-memory changes are flushed to disk (was silently lost on restart)

2. Statement constructor and DatabaseWrapper.prepare() now pass
   filePath so Statement knows where to save

3. _extractTableName handles sqlite_master (routes by WHERE name=)
   and PRAGMA table_info() so table-existence checks hit the right DB

4. closeDualDb() no longer saves the record DB on shutdown —
   it is read-only and must never be written back

Table routing also updated:
- RECORD_TABLES: added game_id_map
- LOCAL_TABLES: added card_display_log, run_state, migrations
